pattern: For each cell with value 1, count how many right triangles use it as the right-angle vertex by multiplying the number of other 1s in its row by the number of other 1s in its column.
hints: Precompute row_count[i] = number of 1s in row i and col_count[j] = number of 1s in column j.
For a cell (i,j) with value 1, it can serve as the right-angle corner; the other two vertices must be another 1 in the same row and another 1 in the same column.
The contribution of cell (i,j) is (row_count[i] - 1) * (col_count[j] - 1); sum over all 1-cells.

edge_inputs.description: three collinear ones in a column — no right triangle
L-shaped grid with ones on border
sparse ones that share no row or column
